steps.title: Spot the parallel structure
steps.content: Immigrants from Mandarin-speaking regions of China settled in Flushing and speak Mandarin; immigrants from Cantonese/Fuzhounese-speaking regions settled in Chinatown and speak those languages. The pattern shows: home-region language shapes where they settle and what they speak.

steps.title: Identify the reason
steps.content:
Choice B directly explains why the settlement pattern mirrors the language distribution: geographic language differences within China are reflected in how Chinese immigrants settled across NYC neighborhoods.

Immigrants naturally clustered with others from their home regions and maintained their original languages.

The other choices introduce unsupported information—individual multilingualism patterns (A), residence changes (C), or population counts (D)—that don't account for the observed language-settlement correlation.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
